---
title: "Getting Started"
description: "Get up and running with jaxcmr"
---

Welcome to jaxcmr, a JAX-accelerated toolkit for modeling memory search using Context Maintenance and Retrieval (CMR) models.

## What is jaxcmr?

jaxcmr provides fast, differentiable implementations of CMR models for:

- **Simulation**: Generate synthetic recall sequences from CMR models
- **Likelihood evaluation**: Compute the probability of observed recall data under a model
- **Fitting**: Estimate model parameters from experimental data using differential evolution
- **Behavioral analyses**: Compute standard memory research metrics (SPC, CRP, etc.)

## Who is this for?

This library is designed for **memory researchers** who want to:

- Model free recall and serial recall data with CMR
- Fit CMR parameters to experimental datasets
- Compare different CMR variants
- Run simulations to generate predictions

## Getting Started

1. [**Installation**](installation.qmd) - Install jaxcmr and its dependencies
2. [**Quickstart**](quickstart.qmd) - Run your first CMR simulation
3. [**Data Format**](data-format.qmd) - Understand how to format your experimental data

## Next Steps

After getting set up, explore:

- [**Concepts**](/concepts/) - Understand how CMR works
- [**Tutorials**](/tutorials/) - Learn to fit models and analyze results
- [**Analyses**](/analyses/) - See all available behavioral analyses
